topic: Part 2b Monitoring Illusion — stratification paragraph SC bucket clause; EXP-489 'monitoring would expose the contradiction' overclaim fix (11/12 derivational)
our_argument_summary: The stratification paragraph in Part 2b bucketizes the 43 monitoring=none WINs by verdict. The SC clause was patched (commit a1fd149, EXP-489) from 'monitoring would expose the contradiction' to 'most of these contradictions are derivational (incompatible geometry, formula identity with standard physics, or missing mechanism) and visible from the dome's own derivation pages without live data; only the small empirical-prediction subset would actually be exposed by monitoring.' This corrects the c1 hole 6 overclaim flagged in SEC-part2b-stratification-paragraph.c1 (ISS-2317).

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: build-scripts/generate-html.js Line 1429: caNoneSC = caNoneBase.filter(verdict==='Self-Contradicted').length where caNoneBase = wins with code_analysis.monitoring==='none'. Computed live from wins.json at every build; no hardcoded count.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Computed. {{CA_NONE_SC}} and {{CA_NONE_SC_IDS}} are template variables substituted from wins.json at build time. The rendered docs/index.html (verified at commit 0f124a8) shows '12 self-contradicted ... (WIN-034, WIN-044, WIN-047, WIN-048, WIN-050, WIN-051, WIN-052, WIN-053, WIN-054, WIN-055, WIN-056, WIN-061)'. Independent recount from wins.json: 12 WINs match verdict=='Self-Contradicted' AND monitoring=='none'; ID list matches exactly.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None. The new prose adds a qualitative concession ('most are derivational') but does not introduce any adaptive logic or threshold.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None in build pipeline. Two metadata discrepancies in the queue push itself — see hole 4.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level review of part2b prose. The 12 underlying SC WINs all carry code_analysis.monitoring==='none' in wins.json — verified by direct grep.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Section-level review. The stratification paragraph itself is meta-discussion of the monitoring stratification — not a relabel of any specific physical claim.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Section-level review. The patch corrects an internal overclaim; it does not adopt a value retroactively.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Section-level review. Counts derive from our wins.json, not from dome geometry.

holes_found.description: Prose says 'most of these contradictions are derivational' but EXP-489's per-WIN analysis (per_win_categorization in monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-489-ISS-2317-sc-monitoring-overclaim.json) is precise: 11 of 12. 'Most' is true but soft — the precise ratio is available and would let a careful reader audit the claim against the listed IDs.
holes_found.recommendation: Sharpen 'most of these contradictions' → '11 of these 12 contradictions' (or '11 out of 12'). Trades one syllable for an auditable number. Same fix could be applied to 'the small empirical-prediction subset' → 'the single empirical-prediction case (WIN-061)' — see hole 2.

holes_found.description: The new prose refers to 'the small empirical-prediction subset would actually be exposed by monitoring' without identifying which WIN(s) qualify. EXP-489 identifies exactly one: WIN-061 (Schumann suppression G3 storm). Readers who try to match the claim against the listed IDs (WIN-034..WIN-061) cannot tell which one is the empirical case. Naming WIN-061 closes the audit gap — the full ID list is already inline immediately before the clause.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'the small empirical-prediction subset' with 'the single empirical-prediction case (WIN-061, Schumann storm suppression)' or similar. Forces the prose to commit to the EXP-489 classification on the page rather than off-page in the expansion file.

holes_found.description: WIN-061 is classified by EXP-489 as 'observational' (the only one), but the wins.json verdict text records two independent contradictions: (a) the dome cavity formula predicts ~22 Hz vs the observed 7.83 Hz — a derivational/formula-identity mismatch in the dome's own publication, not requiring fresh data; (b) the claimed ≥30%-suppression / 6-hour-window threshold has no dome derivation, so checking it requires real G3-storm Schumann data — genuinely observational. The new prose framing ('only the small empirical-prediction subset would actually be exposed by monitoring') reads as if WIN-061 is purely observational, which understates the derivational layer that already refutes the base claim. A dome defender will not exploit this (it's a self-own to do so), but precision matters: even the 'one empirical case' has a derivational foundation. The patch is still net-positive — just slightly more nuanced than the prose admits.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional tightening: add a half-sentence acknowledging the mixed character of WIN-061, e.g., 'only the small empirical-prediction subset (WIN-061, where the dome's published ≥30% suppression threshold has no derivation and must be checked against real storm data) would actually be exposed by monitoring beyond what the dome's own derivation pages already refute.' Or leave as-is and rely on the per-WIN evidence in WIN-061's panel — readers who click through see both layers. Not blocking.

holes_found.description: Queue item 432's context_hints metadata is misattributed. The reason text correctly identifies 'EXP-489: SC monitoring overclaim prose patch applied (11/12 derivational classification)' and the integrating commit a1fd149 commit message correctly cites 'EXP-489 SC monitoring overclaim fixed (ISS-2317)'. But the queue entry's context_hints lists source_file='monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-516-ISS-2373-bracket-strawman.json' and related_issues=['ISS-2373','ISS-2408'] — those belong to EXP-516 (the bracket-strawman fix integrated in earlier commit 1702c23, separately), not EXP-489. Effect: a curmudgeon following the context_hints to find the source EXP would read the wrong file. This is a decider push-metadata defect, not a content defect in the prose itself — flagging for decider visibility so the carry-over template can be fixed.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider follow-up: when pushing multi-EXP-batch commits to the queue, regenerate context_hints per-target rather than carrying over from a prior push. Not a content patch — no sections.json change needed.

stronger_arguments.description: If the analyst/decider want to maximize defender-resistance of this paragraph, the prose could explicitly name the 'how we know' channel per WIN class — e.g., 'theological-etymology (WIN-034), version-instability (WIN-044), missing-mechanism (WIN-047/048/052/054), formula identity with globe physics (WIN-050/051/055/056), self-incompatible derivation (WIN-053).' That maps each SC WIN to its derivational basis on the page itself, removing any 'argument by authority' flavor. Cost: prose becomes longer; gain: zero residual room for a defender to say 'you assert these are derivational without showing your work.' Current EXP-489 prose stops short of this and lives with 'most are derivational' as a summary. Reasonable trade-off either way; flagging the upside.

Defender's strongest counter-attack — already neutralized but worth recording: 'You concede that monitoring would not have exposed 11 of these 12 contradictions, which means monitoring=none is not a fair stratification axis for the SC bucket.' Response on the page already implicit: the monitoring=none signal still flags the dome's refusal to put these WINs under live test even though some (WIN-061) plainly could be monitored. The stratification axis splits the bucket; it doesn't claim that monitoring is the only criterion for SC. The current prose is honest about this. No prose change needed.
sources: data/wins.json (WIN-061 finding: '~22 Hz base frequency; suppression pattern follows ionospheric (globe) physics')

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-489 correctly identifies and corrects a real overclaim in the c1 prose. The old text — 'monitoring would expose the contradiction' applied to all 12 SC-unmonitored WINs — was literally false for the 11 derivational cases (data/wins.json WIN-034 theological, WIN-044 version-instability, WIN-047/048/052/054 missing-mechanism, WIN-050/051/055/056 formula-identity, WIN-053 flux-conservation contradiction; EXP-489-ISS-2317-sc-monitoring-overclaim.json:per_win_categorization). The new prose ('visible from the dome's own derivation pages without live data; only the small empirical-prediction subset would actually be exposed by monitoring') is honest and defensible (data/sections.json part2b.html stratification paragraph). The patch lands cleanly: build-scripts/generate-html.js Line 1429 still resolves CA_NONE_SC=12 against the IDs WIN-034..WIN-061 (docs/index.html line 1233 rendered output verified at commit 0f124a8).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The patch resolves an overclaim hole (ISS-2317, severity=moderate, status=fixed) but does not save or strengthen the underlying SC verdicts on these 12 WINs — those still hold on their own derivational/structural merits (data/wins.json individual finding fields per WIN). The 11/12 reframe is a precision improvement, not a substantive verdict-shifting change. A defender cannot use the patch to challenge any specific SC verdict — only to argue that monitoring stratification is a weaker bucketing axis than verdict stratification for the SC sub-bucket, which the c1 hole already conceded by motivating EXP-489 in the first place.

deeper_analysis: Verification-class review for queue_id 432 (EXP-489 SC monitoring overclaim patch, ISS-2317 closed). Phase 1 verify-mode handoff gates evaluated: (a) class=verification PASS, (b) latest part2b review (stratification.c2) has 1 hole <=2 PASS, (c) all minor PASS, (d) NO applied-patches/*.json since 2026-05-31T03:15:49 FAIL — this item stays in scope per gate (d). Independent recount of SC + monitoring=none WINs in data/wins.json confirms 12, IDs match the rendered docs/index.html line 1233 output exactly. Independent verification of EXP-489's 11/12 derivational classification against wins.json finding/detail_verdict_text fields — all 11 derivational classifications check out (theological etymology for WIN-034; version-instability for WIN-044; missing-mechanism for WIN-047 Hubble, WIN-048 CMB Axis, WIN-052 RAR, WIN-054 El Gordo; formula identity for WIN-050/051 tidal periods, WIN-055/056 distance-redshift/solar-elevation; self-incompatible derivation for WIN-053 ring magnet flux conservation). WIN-061 classified observational is defensible because the claimed ≥30%-suppression threshold has no dome derivation, requiring real G3-storm data to test — though the 22 Hz vs 7.83 Hz baseline-frequency mismatch is a derivational layer the prose does not surface. Patch correctness: integrating commit a1fd149 (decider 2026-05-31T20-08) applies the targeted string replacement in data/sections.json part2b.html; rendered output at commit 0f124a8 shows the new text live in docs/index.html. ISS-2317 is now status=fixed in monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json. Net: patch is sound and content holds; the 4 minor holes are precision/audit observations, not substantive defects.

citations_verified: data/wins.json: 12 SC + monitoring=none WINs (recount verified, IDs match)
data/sections.json part2b.html stratification paragraph (new prose present at clone HEAD 0f124a8)
docs/index.html line 1233 (rendered output 12 self-contradicted with correct ID list)
git commit a1fd149 message (cites EXP-489 SC monitoring overclaim fixed, ISS-2317)
citations_unchecked: Whether the dome's own derivation pages truly contain visible-without-live-data refutations for all 11 derivational WINs — assumed accurate per prior cycles' raw-text reviews; not re-verified in this run.

summary_for_decider: Queue item 432 (part2b, class=verification): EXP-489 SC monitoring overclaim patch landed correctly in commit a1fd149. Rendered prose, template substitution, and counts all verify. Four minor holes: (1) tighten 'most' to '11 of 12' (audit-friendly precision); (2) name WIN-061 as the single empirical case; (3) acknowledge WIN-061's mixed character (optional); (4) decider push-metadata defect — context_hints carried over from EXP-516 push instead of regenerated for EXP-489. None are blocking. recommended_action=minor_edit. SC verdicts on the 12 underlying WINs unaffected. ISS-2317 is correctly closed.
